title: The Squeaky Quest for the Balloon Palace
summary: Seeker and his companion Squeaky the balloon dog embark on a magical journey to Balloon World to return a balloon cat to her home. After navigating a landscape filled with balloon buildings and enduring funny static electricity, they visit the magnificent red Balloon Palace. There, they meet the Balloon King and reunite Squeaky the cat with her animal friends, including birds, parrots, and mice. Their adventure expands as Sana the orange balloon duck joins them, eager to learn about the human world and the mysterious rituals of bedtime.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Now it's time for stars and stories. Who should we tell a story about?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dimitar Seeker... oh who is it Seeker? It's Squeaky, I heard that Izzy had a balloon and I want to bring the balloon to balloon world.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh hi Squeaky. Well, um, I think we could do that. Come on in... while I search for our balloon world potion, would you like any, um, snacks?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I would like some crackers.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I didn't know balloon dogs liked crackers. Well, here's some Ritz if you like those.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I do like them. Munch munch crunch crunch.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Now, does this balloon that you've got want to go back to balloon world or is it happy in our world?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Ah, well it came here from balloon world and it kind of likes it, but he thinks balloon world.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay. Well, let me search in my potion closet. And Seeker looked around and said, oh, hmm, this is... oh no, that's not... that's the smokers potion... no, there's the floofacloud potion, a mucky yucky one, gemstone reef. No, what else? Hmm, oh, I wonder what this green one is. We'll find out someday. I got a brown one, blue one... oh, here we go. It's this bright red shiny one and it smells a little rubbery. I think that's it. Now let's both take a sip, Squeaky. And now you may say the rhyme.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Squeak, squeak, squeak, squeak, squeak, squeak.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And then there was a... pffft... a big squeak. Seeker and Squeaky appeared in both Balloon World along with their orange balloon that they had brought.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Now we need to go to balloon king.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, the balloon king. Hmm. Where is that balloon palace? And Seeker looked around and said, I see the balloon restaurant and the balloon school... and oh, there's the balloon library where they have all kinds of balloon books. Um, not sure how to get to the palace though. We better ask somebody for directions.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh, how about we ask the balloon guard?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh. Oh, hello balloon guard, said Seeker.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Balloon guard! So the balloon guard is the king... balloon guard, how do we get to the king's palace?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And the balloon guard says, 'Oh yes, just go past the balloon library and up the balloon hill and you'll see the palace at the top. Have a good day.' And so Seeker and Squeaky went um past the library and up the balloon hill. And you know what happened while they were walking up the balloon hill? Their feet made a funny sound on the balloon path, which was made of balloons.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Squeak, squeak, squeak, squeak, squeak, squeak.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Seeker had long hair on top of his head and also a big bushy beard. And because they were walking on the balloons and squeaking along on them, you know what happened?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: What?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: They made a lot of static electricity. And so what, what do you think happened to Seeker's beard and his hair?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Nothing.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It stood straight up in the air! And he looked a little bit silly. And Seeker said, 'Wow, balloon world must be really staticy.' And Squeaky the balloon dog said, 'Yeah, doesn't really bother us most of the time because we're balloons.' And Seeker said, 'Yeah, I guess you don't really have hair to worry about.' And Squeaky the balloon dog said, 'Yeah, I wish we had some hair, but we don't.' And so they made it to the palace.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I wish somebody would brush my fur, but Seeker said you don't have any fur. And Squeaky said, 'Oh yeah, that's okay.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And what did the balloon palace before them look like?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It was so big big big and tall but then who did they see?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, at first they didn't see anyone. Just the huge palace, which was a kind of castle. And instead of stone, all of the walls were made of balloons. And were they all one color or were they many different colors?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They were only red.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow, the balloon palace was made of only red balloons. It must have been a beautiful palace. And how many towers did it have?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: One, two, three, four, five, six.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Six towers. And what was at the top of the towers? Did they have like pointy roofs or were they flat on top for people to walk around?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Flat.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: They were flat on top. And did it have a very big door in the front or a little door?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: This big.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A door this big in the front.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Um, we just knocked or did we have a doorbell or could we just walk right in? We just knock.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh. And what sound did it make?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Boom boom boom. Like that, when you knock on the balloon.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And so Seeker and Squeaky stood there and Seeker knocked on the balloon door and then a deep voice from inside the balloon palace said, 'Who goes there?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh, it's us, Seeker and Squeaky. We brought a balloon to your castle.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: You may enter! And then there was a big... squeak... and the balloon doors opened up and they saw the royal guard who was just a very big person made out of balloons and he said, 'Please come with me, the king awaits.' And so they walked with the balloon guard down a long hallway and into a big room with a tall ceiling called the throne room. And at the end of the room was a very big chair made of balloons. And were they all red too?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yep.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, they were all red. And sitting in the balloon throne made of red balloons was the Balloon King. And the Balloon King said, 'Welcome travelers. I am the Balloon King. And you must be Seeker and Squeaky. Come and say hello to me.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hello.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And what have you brought?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: We had brought a balloon.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And who is this balloon that you have brought?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's Squeaky the balloon cat.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Squeaky the balloon dog said, 'That's a balloon cat? I didn't know that.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah. Meow meow meow.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And the Balloon King said, 'We must let Squeaky the balloon cat speak for herself. What do you have to say for yourself, Squeaky the balloon cat?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I have to say that that I miss my balloon friends. My balloon friends are: Bitsy the balloon bird, Kazoo the balloon parrot, Squeegee and Squeaky the mouse.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A lot of balloon creatures named Squeaky, I love it. And the king said, 'I see. And why must you miss them instead of being with them?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I just... I just... I really want to explore the... the... people world... and and then I discovered a fact that I missed my friends here in balloon world.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I see. Well, welcome back to balloon world, said the Balloon King. But before you go and see your friends, please tell me of your adventures in people world.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I adventured to Izzy's house.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Who is this Izzy?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She's a person.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Is she a friend of balloons or does she pop them?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh, she is a friend of balloons.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And what does she do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She just like, she... her favorite animals are cats and fish and she's really into sharks, leopard seals.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I see. And did you have a fun time with her and learn things together?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yep.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What did you learn?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Um, I learned... I first I... when I was doing balloon science today, right before bed when I was trying to see static from this orange balloon, um, she really she really didn't see any static coming out of the balloon.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I see. Sometimes balloons make static and sometimes they do not. That's why you must always continue to try the experiment to see if the results changes. And what else did you learn in your experiments?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Um, I think... I think... Seeker had an Izzy's house potion.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Uh, yes, that's true, said Seeker.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And the king said, 'Would you like to return to your house, Seeker?' And Seeker said, 'Well, um, I think as long as we've returned Squeaky the cat balloon to her friends, um, I can go home, yes.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes, but what about taking you to see Izzy's house or taking some of the balloons to just have a little quick visit?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Hmm, who do you think would like to visit Izzy's house, said the king.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Sana the balloon duck.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What was her name?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Sana. Sana the balloon duck.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And the king said, 'Who's that?' But before he'd even finished speaking, you know what they heard?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She's... she's made of balloons. Quack quack quack quack quack quack quack.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And it was Sana coming waddling quickly into the throne room. And Sana was a beautiful duck, all made of balloons. And what color was Sana?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Sana was orange. Sana, you really didn't want to visit Izzy's house? Quack quack quack quack quack.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And the king said, 'Uh, that's just a duck, it doesn't really...' but Seeker said, 'Oh, actually, uh, I speak duck.' And I could tell that Sana was saying, 'I don't know what Izzy's house is, but I'm excited to go on an adventure and I'd like to go with you.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Okay. Quack quack.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Seeker said, 'Yes, that's true. Quack quack quack quack quack quack quack.' And Sana said, 'Quack quack quack quack quack.' And Seeker said, 'Quack quack, quack quack quack quack quack.' And the king said, 'Well, uh, you can have your duck quacking conversation somewhere else. Thank you, very nice to meet you everyone. Goodbye.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And so Seeker and Squeaky the balloon cat and Sana the balloon duck took the going home potion so that they could all go to Seeker's house first. And then Seeker said, 'Hmm, somewhere I've got an Izzy's house potion so Sana the duck balloon could visit my house.' And Sana the duck said, 'Quack quack quack quack quack.' And Seeker said, 'Oh, you're right. I think that that will have to wait for another story.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And that's the end of stars and stories.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh, what about this... you can pretend this can be Sana the duck balloon.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, next time we could do that. That's a good idea.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, no, I want to do that today right now.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, but it's almost time to go to sleep.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, I just... I just want to tell him the rest of my bedtime.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, um, well let's ask Sana. Sana the balloon duck, would you like to learn about Izzy's bedtime? Quack quack quack quack quack. Oh, and Seeker said that means yes. Please tell me what the next parts of bedtime are.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Diaper shirt, then bed. Quack.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And that meant okay. Sana is ready to observe bedtime.
